title: Cheesy Borek
description: A classic Turkish pastry filled with cheese and served as a snack or light meal. This recipe uses a simple dough and a flavorful cheese filling.
instructions: Roll out the borek dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut the dough into squares of about 4 inches per side.
Place a tablespoon of cheese in the center of each square.
Brush the edges with water and fold the dough over the cheese to form a triangle.
Press the edges together to seal the borek.
Brush the tops with a little bit of water and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve warm with a side of garlicky spread and mushroom duxelles.
